---
title: "Analyzing E-commerce Consumer Behaviour"
author: "Phil (Fei Han) & Doniyorbek Komilov"
format: pdf
editor: visual
date: "2026-04-16"
---

```{r}
# Load libraries
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library(tidyverse)
library(corrplot)
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(car)
library(pROC)
library(DHARMa)
library(ResourceSelection)
library(mgcv)
library(arm)
})

# --- Clean and Preprocess Data ---
df_clean <- read.csv("online_shoppers_intention.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert Revenue to Factor
    Revenue = factor(Revenue, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No Purchase", "Purchase")),
    
    # Convert Month (Define Order: Feb is the Reference Level)
    Month = factor(Month, levels = c("Feb", "Mar", "May", "June", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec")),
    
    # Convert VisitorType and Weekend
    VisitorType = as.factor(VisitorType),
    Weekend = factor(Weekend, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("Weekday", "Weekend")),
    
    # Create Numeric Label for calculations
    is_purchase = as.numeric(Revenue == "Purchase")
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

# Define a consistent theme for the project
project_theme <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: data-summary
#| message: false

# Check for missing values
cat("Missing values in dataset:", sum(is.na(df_clean)), "\n")

# View the distribution of our target variable: Revenue
table(df_clean$Revenue)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-class-imbalance
#| fig-cap: "Class Imbalance in Target Variable"

df_clean %>%
  count(Revenue) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Revenue, y = pct, fill = Revenue)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct * 100, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF6666", "#00CC66")) +
  labs(title = "Class Imbalance: Purchase vs. No Purchase",
       subtitle = "Dataset is heavily skewed toward non-purchase sessions",
       x = "Transaction Outcome", y = "Proportion") +
  project_theme
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-pagevalues
#| fig-cap: "Comparison of Page Values for Purchase vs. No Purchase"

ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = Revenue, y = PageValues, fill = Revenue)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF6666", "#00CC66")) +
  labs(
    title = "Does Page Value Predict Purchase?",
    subtitle = "Higher PageValues are strongly associated with successful transactions",
    x = "Transaction Outcome (Revenue)",
    y = "Page Value Score"
  ) +
  project_theme
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-monthly-trend
#| fig-cap: "Monthly Conversion Rates Throughout the Year"

df_clean %>% 
  group_by(Month) %>% 
  summarise(Conversion_Rate = mean(Revenue == "Purchase")) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = Conversion_Rate, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkblue") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "E-commerce Seasonality: Monthly Conversion Rates",
    x = "Month of the Year",
    y = "Conversion Rate (%)"
  ) +
  project_theme
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-visitor-type
#| fig-cap: "Purchase Proportion by Visitor Type"

ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = VisitorType, fill = Revenue)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Visitor Behaviour: New vs. Returning",
    x = "Visitor Category",
    y = "Percentage",
    fill = "Purchased?"
  ) +
  project_theme
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-correlation
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-width: 7

# Select numeric columns and calculate correlation
numeric_df <- df_clean %>% select_if(is.numeric)
cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_df)

corrplot::corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "circle", type = "upper", 
                   tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45, 
                   title = "Correlation Heatmap of Website Metrics",
                   mar = c(0,0,1,0))
```

```{r}
#| label: data-cleaning
#| message: false

df <- read.csv("online_shoppers_intention.csv")

df_clean <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Revenue = factor(Revenue, 
                     levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), 
                     labels = c("No Purchase", "Purchase")),
    
    Month = factor(Month, 
                   levels = c("Feb", "Mar", "May", "June", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec")),
    
    VisitorType = as.factor(VisitorType),
    Weekend = factor(Weekend, 
                     levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), 
                     labels = c("Weekday", "Weekend")),
    
    is_purchase = as.numeric(Revenue == "Purchase")
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

```

```{r}
summary(df_clean)


```

```{r}
#| label: train-test-split

set.seed(42)

n <- nrow(df_clean)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = 0.8 * n)

train_data <- df_clean[train_idx, ]
test_data  <- df_clean[-train_idx, ]
train_data$log_PageValues <- log1p(train_data$PageValues)
train_data$log_ExitRates  <- log1p(train_data$ExitRates)
train_data$log_ProductRelated_Duration <- log1p(train_data$ProductRelated_Duration)

test_data$log_PageValues <- log1p(test_data$PageValues)
test_data$log_ExitRates  <- log1p(test_data$ExitRates)
test_data$log_ProductRelated_Duration <- log1p(test_data$ProductRelated_Duration)

df_clean$log_PageValues <- log1p(df_clean$PageValues)
df_clean$log_ExitRates  <- log1p(df_clean$ExitRates)
df_clean$log_ProductRelated_Duration <- log1p(df_clean$ProductRelated_Duration)
cat("Training set size:", nrow(train_data), "\n")
cat("Test set size:", nrow(test_data), "\n")
cat("Training set purchase rate:", round(mean(train_data$is_purchase), 3), "\n")
cat("Purchase rate of test set:", round(mean(test_data$is_purchase), 3), "\n")
```

```{r}
train_data <- read.csv("online_shoppers_intention.csv")


minority_count <- sum(train_data$Revenue == TRUE)
majority_count <- sum(train_data$Revenue == FALSE) 
train_data$class_weight <- ifelse(train_data$Revenue == TRUE, 
                                  majority_count/minority_count, 
                                  1) 

final_formula <- Revenue ~ Administrative + ProductRelated + BounceRates + ExitRates + Month + VisitorType
glm_balanced <- glm(final_formula,
                    data    = train_data,
                    family  = quasibinomial,
                    weights = class_weight, 
                    control = glm.control(maxit = 200))


library(pROC)
pred_prob <- predict(glm_balanced, type = "response")  
roc_curve <- roc(train_data$Revenue, pred_prob)  
auc(roc_curve)  
```

```{r}
prob_glm <- predict(glm_balanced, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
roc_glm  <- roc(test_data$is_purchase, prob_glm)
cat("Holdout GLM AUC:", round(auc(roc_glm), 4), "\n")
plot(roc_glm, col = "#2c3e50", lwd = 3,
     main = paste("ROC Curve (GLM)\nAUC =", round(auc(roc_glm), 3)))
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")
```

```{r}
vif_values <- vif(glm_balanced)

print(vif_values)
```

```{r}
exp_coefficients <- exp(coef(glm_balanced))

print(exp_coefficients)
```

```{r}

diag_model <- glm(final_formula,
                  data   = train_data,
                  family = binomial,
                  control = glm.control(maxit = 100))

binnedplot(fitted(diag_model), 
           residuals(diag_model, type = "response"),
           nclass = 40,
           main = "Binned Residual Plot (GLM)",
           xlab = "Fitted Values",
           ylab = "Average Residuals",
           col.int = "grey")
```

```{r}

all_cols <- colnames(train_data)
cat("train_data：", paste(all_cols, collapse=", "), "\n")

n_purchase <- sum(train_data$Revenue == TRUE)
n_no_purchase <- sum(train_data$Revenue == FALSE)
train_data$case_weight <- ifelse(
  train_data$Revenue == TRUE, 
  n_no_purchase / n_purchase, 
  1
)

gam_model <- gam(
  Revenue ~ s(PageValues) + s(ExitRates) + s(ProductRelated_Duration) + 
    Month + OperatingSystems + VisitorType + Weekend,
  family = quasibinomial,
  data = train_data, 
  weights = case_weight
)

gam.check(gam_model)
summary(gam_model)
```

```{r}
plot(gam_model, pages = 1, shade = TRUE, seWithMean = TRUE, main = "GAM Smoothing Curves")
```

```{r}

set.seed(42)
k      <- 5
folds  <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = nrow(df_clean)))
auc_glm_cv <- numeric(k)
auc_gam_cv <- numeric(k)

for (i in 1:k) {
  cv_train <- df_clean[folds != i, ]
  cv_test  <- df_clean[folds == i, ]

  np <- sum(cv_train$Revenue == TRUE)
  nn <- sum(cv_train$Revenue == FALSE)
  cv_train$case_weight <- ifelse(cv_train$Revenue == TRUE, nn / np, 1)

  cv_test$case_weight <- 1

  cv_glm <- glm(final_formula,
                data    = cv_train,
                family  = quasibinomial,
                weights = case_weight,
                control = glm.control(maxit = 200))

  cv_gam <- gam(Revenue ~ s(PageValues) + s(ExitRates) + s(ProductRelated_Duration) +
                  Month + OperatingSystems + VisitorType + Weekend,
                family  = quasibinomial,
                data    = cv_train,
                weights = case_weight)

  p_glm <- predict(cv_glm, newdata = cv_test, type = "response")
  p_gam <- predict(cv_gam, newdata = cv_test, type = "response")

  auc_glm_cv[i] <- as.numeric(auc(roc(cv_test$Revenue, p_glm, quiet = TRUE)))
  auc_gam_cv[i] <- as.numeric(auc(roc(cv_test$Revenue, p_gam, quiet = TRUE)))
}

cat("GLM CV AUC: mean =", round(mean(auc_glm_cv), 4),
    " sd =", round(sd(auc_glm_cv), 4), "\n")
cat("GAM CV AUC: mean =", round(mean(auc_gam_cv), 4),
    " sd =", round(sd(auc_gam_cv), 4), "\n")

cv_results <- data.frame(
  Fold  = rep(1:k, 2),
  AUC   = c(auc_glm_cv, auc_gam_cv),
  Model = rep(c("GLM", "GAM"), each = k)
)

ggplot(cv_results, aes(x = factor(Fold), y = AUC, color = Model, group = Model)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(AUC), color = Model), linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "5-Fold Cross-Validation AUC: GLM vs GAM",
       x = "Fold", y = "AUC") 

model_comparison <- data.frame(
  Model       = c("GLM (quasibinomial)", "GAM (quasibinomial)"),
  CV_AUC_mean = c(round(mean(auc_glm_cv), 4), round(mean(auc_gam_cv), 4)),
  CV_AUC_sd   = c(round(sd(auc_glm_cv),  4), round(sd(auc_gam_cv),  4))
)
print(model_comparison)
```

```{r}
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)

gam_vars <- all.vars(terms(gam_model)) %>% setdiff("Revenue")
missing_vars <- setdiff(gam_vars, colnames(test_data))
if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
  stop("The test set is missing required variables
：", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", "))
} else {
  cat("✅he trial set variables are complete, and the analysis can continue
n")
}

test_data$Weekend <- as.logical(test_data$Weekend)
train_data$Weekend <- as.logical(train_data$Weekend)

```

```{r}
library(pROC)

test_data$Weekend <- as.logical(test_data$Weekend)

prob_glm <- predict(glm_balanced, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
prob_gam <- predict(gam_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

prob_glm_train <- predict(glm_balanced, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
prob_gam_train <- predict(gam_model, newdata = train_data, type = "response")

roc_glm <- roc(train_data$Revenue, prob_glm_train, quiet=T)
roc_gam <- roc(train_data$Revenue, prob_gam_train, quiet=T)

cat("GLM AUC:", round(auc(roc_glm),4), "\n")
cat("GAM AUC:", round(auc(roc_gam),4), "\n")

plot(roc_glm, col="red", lwd=3, main="ROC Curve GLM vs GAM")
plot(roc_gam, col="blue", lwd=3, add=T)
legend("bottomright",
       c(paste0("GLM AUC=",round(auc(roc_glm),4)),
         paste0("GAM AUC=",round(auc(roc_gam),4))),
       col=c("red","blue"), lwd=3)
```
